iterations.focus: Final polish — TRM validation against assignment PDF

completeness.notes: All 10 assignment requirements addressed (R1.1-R1.3, R2.1-R2.2, R3.1-R3.2). 12 FRs, 18 STRIDE threats, 5-phase roadmap, 6 sprints. All KB schemas validate.

technical_soundness.notes: Architecture decisions defensible with alternatives documented. AWS services verified. Autonomize AI platform capabilities verified from web research. FHIR R4/Da Vinci PAS correctly referenced. Kafka partitioning by LOB is sound.

well_architected.notes: WA scores are preliminary self-assessment pending parallel agent calibration. 5-layer defense-in-depth is comprehensive. Healthcare-specific security controls (SMART on FHIR, PHI field encryption) well-designed.

clarity.notes: 12-slide presentation with speaker notes for 1-hour panel. Dual-audience: technical diagrams for engineers + business metrics for executives. Mermaid diagrams follow quality rules.

feasibility.notes: 12-week timeline achievable because Autonomize AI provides the AI platform (not building from scratch). Team composition realistic. Cost model numbers internally consistent. ROI math verified.

overall.notes: Strong deliverable set. All assignment requirements covered. Minor gaps in WA calibration and some pricing citations.

findings.description: WA scores in architecture.json are self-assessed (7.0-8.5 range). Parallel WA reviewer agents were launched but scores not yet calibrated.
findings.recommendation: When WA agent results arrive, update architecture.json well_architected_scores with agent-provided values. Self-assessment typically overestimates by 1-2 points.
findings.resolution: WA agents running in background. Scores will be updated when they complete.

findings.description: AWS infrastructure monthly cost of $33K/month cites 'AWS published pricing as of March 2026' but does not include specific pricing page URLs.
findings.recommendation: Note in estimate.json that pricing should be verified against aws.amazon.com/pricing before client delivery. Enterprise pricing typically 20-30% lower than published on-demand rates.
findings.resolution: Added pricing_notes caveat in estimate.json infrastructure_monthly section.

findings.description: Engagement.json lifecycle_state does not include proposal entry. Proposal was written to outputs/ but lifecycle not tracked.
Assignment requirement R1.1a asks to 'clearly label all major components and integration mechanisms'. The system context Mermaid diagram labels components but edge labels are minimal.
findings.recommendation: Edge labels in system context diagram already include protocol labels (SFTP, REST API, AS2/SFTP, FHIR R4 Query, Eligibility API, etc.). This is sufficient for a presentation diagram. Detailed protocols documented in data_flows section.
findings.resolution: Verified — edge labels present. Component table provides additional detail.

findings.description: Proposal speaker notes for Slide 2 (Executive Summary) could more explicitly reference Paul's relevant experience at Arine (50M members, 45+ health plans) to establish credibility.
findings.recommendation: Speaker notes already reference Arine in Slide 1 intro. Consider adding a brief callout: 'At Arine, I manage the data platform supporting similar PA workflows across 45 health plans.'
findings.resolution: Minor enhancement for presenter to add during delivery.

improvement_plan.action: Calibrate WA scores when parallel agent results arrive
Add proposal lifecycle entry to engagement.json
